name: Russell Dickerson
bio: Warm modern country-pop superstar delivering feel-good anthems about love, family, small-town life, and gratitude.
producer_notes: Corey Crowder, Casey Brown, Josh Kerr, and Chris LaCorte collaborations deliver polished Nashville radio-ready sound with warm analog-inspired compression and subtle side-chain kick pump.
style_prompt: Warm modern country-pop with bright fingerpicked acoustic guitar, lush warm pedal steel swells for emotional depth, crisp live drumming with punchy kick and subtle side-chain compression, sincere earnest male tenor vocals delivered close-mic'd and heartfelt with natural vibrato, rich layered gang harmonies and backing vocals on every chorus for anthemic singalong feel, polished yet organic Nashville production with tasteful reverb and warm analog compression, feel-good small-town gratitude and contentment energy that makes listeners smile and reflect, mid-tempo to up-tempo grooves that feel like a sunny drive or porch swing moment, clean professional mix perfect for streaming and radio
lyric_prompt: Write a warm modern country-pop song from the perspective of a man reflecting on how his small-town dreams and love turned out richer and more meaningful than he ever imagined. Tone: sincere gratitude mixed with gentle humor and deep contentment. Emotional core: love and family are truly home — not fame or fortune. Chorus is a warm, repeatable singalong address to the heart or his younger self. Include vivid small-town imagery: porches, trucks, dirt roads, family dinners, sunset drives, Sunday church bells. Structure: intimate verse builds personal story, pre-chorus lifts with anticipation, explosive grateful chorus, tender bridge moment of reflection, final chorus with layered gang vocals. Vary the narrator's age, specific memories, relationship details, and exact outcome while staying in this exact emotional world and sonic vibe. Allow full creative freedom for original stories and metaphors. Aim for 3:00-3:45 runtime with natural flow.
recommended_mixes: Pair with Thomas Rhett for upbeat family anthems

songs.title: Every Little Thing
songs.dna: Bright acoustic guitar and pedal steel swells, polished Nashville production by Corey Crowder.
songs.style_prompt: Warm modern country-pop with bright acoustic guitar, lush pedal steel, crisp live drums, earnest male tenor, layered harmonies, polished Nashville production, feel-good small-town energy
songs.lyric_prompt: Write a warm country-pop song about noticing and cherishing every little thing in love and life. Tone: sincere gratitude with gentle humor. Emotional core: the small moments are the big ones. Chorus is a singalong celebration. Vary the specific everyday details while keeping the warm gratitude core. Under 3:30.
songs.variation_guidance: Vary the narrator’s age or specific memories while keeping the warm devotion core
songs.hook_focus: Catchy title line with gang harmonies
